item.author: Rod J. Rohrich, MD
item.title: Woman Saves Twin Sister with Donated Skin: Plastic Surgery Hot Topics with Rod J. Rohrich, MD
item.description:
所属期数: Ahead of Print
出版时间: Tue, 26 Oct 2021 18:37:24 GMT-05:00

In this video, Rod J. Rohrich, MD, Editor-in-Chief of “Plastic and Reconstructive Surgery,” discusses the story of a woman with a rare skin cancer who received a DIEP flap transplanted from her twin sister. This wound up being one of the few vascularized composite allotransplantations between identical twins, and the only reported successful VCA for a recurrent cancer diagnosis.

Read the November 2021 PRS #PlasticSurgery article, “Successful Double DIEP Syngeneic Transplantation across Monozygotic Twins for Total Back Reconstruction” by Selber, Kameni, Torres, Mericli, Chu, Schaverien, Largo, Butler, and Gaber. Find it on PRSJournal.com.

For consumer information on plastic surgery, and to find a board-certified plastic surgeon in your area visit http://plasticsurgery.org

Keywords: plastic surgery; reconstructive surgery; twins; donor; monozygomatic twins; syngeneic transplantation; back reconstruction; DIEP flap; double DIEP; cancer;

item.author: Rod J. Rohrich, MD
item.title: Preventing Witch’s Chin after Genioplasty: Plastic Surgery Hot Topics with Rod J. Rohrich, MD
item.description:
所属期数: Ahead of Print
出版时间: Tue, 26 Oct 2021 18:49:33 GMT-05:00

Witch’s chin deformity is not common after osseous genioplasty (chin augmentation), but it can be difficult to treat if it occurs. That’s why surgeons prefer to prevent it from happening in the first place.

In this video, Rod J. Rohrich, MD, Editor-in-Chief of “Plastic and Reconstructive Surgery,” discusses a new study that compares traditional and “non-degloving technique” osseous genioplasty to determine which provides best outcomes and reduces complications.

Read the November 2021 PRS #PlasticSurgery article, “Osseous Genioplasty: Prevention of Witch’s Chin Deformity with No-Degloving Technique” by Zhang, Byrd, Bradley, Volpicelli, Gibstein, Fisher, Lee, and Bradley. Find it on PRSJournal.com.

For consumer information on plastic surgery, and to find a board-certified plastic surgeon in your area visit http://plasticsurgery.org

Keywords: plastic surgery; cosmetic surgery; witchs chin; chin augmentation; chin surgery; genioplasty; osseous genioplasty; witchs chin deformity;

item.author: Rod J. Rohrich, MD
item.title: African Americans in Breast Recon Social Media: Plastic Surgery Hot Topics with Rod J. Rohrich, MD
item.description:
所属期数: Ahead of Print
出版时间: Wed, 24 Nov 2021 15:31:14 GMT-06:00

Recent data shows that fewer African American women are getting breast reconstruction after cancer. Rod J. Rohrich, MD, Editor-in-Chief of “Plastic and Reconstructive Surgery,” discusses a new study that analyzed plastic surgeons’ social media posts that highlight the procedure to see how well this group of patients is represented.  

Read the December 2021 PRS #PlasticSurgery article, “Representation of African American Patients in Social Media for Breast Reconstruction” by Hassan, Chappell, Ketheeswaran, et al. Find it on PRSJournal.com.

 

For consumer information on plastic surgery, and to find a board-certified plastic surgeon in your area visit http://plasticsurgery.org

Keywords: plastic surgery; breast; breast surgery; breast cancer; African Americans; social media; representation; breast reconstruction; social media influencers;

item.author: Rod J. Rohrich, MD
item.title: What Women Think of DTI Prepectoral Breast Reconstruction: Plastic Surgery Hot Topics with Rod J. Rohrich, MD
item.description:
所属期数: Ahead of Print
出版时间: Wed, 24 Nov 2021 16:11:52 GMT-06:00

Direct-to-implant pre-pec breast reconstruction following mastectomy has recently experienced a surge in popularity among surgeons, but how do patients recovering from breast cancer really feel about this procedure?

In this video, Rod J. Rohrich, MD, Editor-in-Chief of “Plastic and Reconstructive Surgery,” discusses a new study that answers that question by using the Breast-Q tool to find out how women truly felt about the outcomes of their DTI prepectoral breast reconstructions.

Read the December 2021 PRS #PlasticSurgery article, “Direct-to-Implant Prepectoral Breast Reconstruction: Patient-Reported Outcomes” by Safran, Al-Halabi, Viezel-Mathieu, Hazan, and Dionisopoulos. Find it on PRSJournal.com.

For consumer information on plastic surgery, and to find a board-certified plastic surgeon in your area visit http://plasticsurgery.org

Keywords: plastic surgery; cosmetic surgery; prepectoral breast reconstruction; breast reconstruction; breast implants; mastectomy; breast cancer; DTI;

item.author: Rod J. Rohrich, MD
item.title: Treating a Rare HA Fillers Complication: Plastic Surgery Hot Topics with Rod J. Rohrich, MD
item.description:
所属期数: Ahead of Print
出版时间: Wed, 24 Nov 2021 16:21:22 GMT-06:00

Hyaluronic acid fillers are the second-most popular non-invasive aesthetic procedure globally. It’s a simple, out-patient treatment with great results and very low risk of complications.  However, one of those rare complications is pretty serious: intravascular embolisms, or blockage of the blood vessels.

In this video, Rod J. Rohrich, MD, Editor-in-Chief of “Plastic and Reconstructive Surgery,” discusses a new experiment that tested the efficacy of different-sized doses of hyaluronidase, an enzyme which can break down hyaluronic acid and dissolve the embolism.

Read the December 2021 PRS #PlasticSurgery article, “Hyaluronic Acid Embolism Treated with Subcutaneous High and Low Hyaluronidase Doses: Efficacy and Surrounding Tissue Effect” by Salinas-Alvarez, Welsh, Soto-Dominguez, et al. Find it on PRSJournal.com.

For consumer information on plastic surgery, and to find a board-certified plastic surgeon in your area visit http://plasticsurgery.org

Keywords: plastic surgery; experimental; embolism; hyaluronidase; hyaluronic acid; HA; fillers; facial fillers; cosmetic; aesthetic;

item.author: Jesse C. Selber, M.D., M.P.H., F.A.C.S., M.H.C.M
item.title: Robotic Rectus Abdominis Muscle Flap (RoboRAM) Surgery. Video from “Robotic Rectus Abdominis Muscle Flap (RoboRAM) Following Robotic Extirpative Surgery.” 148 (6).
item.description:
所属期数: Ahead of Print
出版时间: Wed, 24 Nov 2021 17:52:13 GMT-06:00

Video demonstrates the peritoneum overlying the deep inferior epigastric artery is opened and the posterior rectus sheath is incised lateral to the linea alba. The muscle is harvested along its entire length in a plane between the muscle and the posterior rectus sheath. The muscle is then dissected off the anterior rectus sheath. Following full dissection of the muscle, the cephalic and caudal margins of the muscle are divided using monopolar scissors. The muscle flap, is then transferred into the pelvis. Finally, the posterior rectus sheath is closed using running barbed suture. The video has been edited to double-speed. From “Robotic Rectus Abdominis Muscle Flap (RoboRAM) Following Robotic Extirpative Surgery.” Plastic and Reconstructive Surgery: December 2021 - 148(6).

Keywords: robotic; Rectus; Abdominis; muscle flap; RoboRAM; surgery; plastic; reconstruction; reconstructive; robot; Innovation; VRAM; APR

item.author: Rod J. Rohrich, MD
item.title: PrePec v. SubPec- What Women Think: Plastic Surgery Hot Topics with Rod J. Rohrich, MD
item.description:
所属期数: Ahead of Print
出版时间: Tue, 25 Jan 2022 16:36:57 GMT-06:00

There are two major types of direct-to-implant breast reconstruction: subpectoral and prepectoral. But which one do women prefer?

In this video, Rod J. Rohrich, MD, Editor-in-Chief of "Plastic and Reconstructive Surgery," discusses a new study that asked over 100 women how the two techniques stacked up.

Read the November 2021 PRS #PlasticSurgery article, "A Comparison of BREAST-Q Scores between Prepectoral and Subpectoral Direct-to-Implant Breast Reconstruction" by Le, Persing, Dinis, Gabrick, Wu, Sinnott, Avraham, Young, and Alperovich. Find it on PRSJournal.com.

For consumer information on plastic surgery, and to find a board-certified plastic surgeon in your area visit http://plasticsurgery.org

Keywords: plastic surgery; breast; breast reconstruction; BREAST-Q; prepectoral; subpectoral; breast surgery; breast cancer; direct-to-implant breast reconstruction;

item.description:
所属期数: Ahead of Print
出版时间: Wed, 23 Feb 2022 16:53:45 GMT-06:00

Video shows in a 47-year-old woman patient the breast volume was reduced exclusively by aspirating 550 ml of fat per side while removing the extra skin. There is no doubt that without reducing the skin envelope, already redundant preoperatively, the ptosis would have worsened. Postoperative result 12 months after surgery. From “Lipo-reduction: A Faster and Safer Breast Remodeling Technique.” Plastic and Reconstructive Surgery: March 2022 - 149 (3).

Keywords: breast reduction breast enlargement liposuction nipple areolar complex mastoplasty

In the left breast 640 ml by liposuction and 150 g by surgical excision. Postoperative result 12 months after surgery. From “Lipo-reduction: A Faster and Safer Breast Remodeling Technique.” Plastic and Reconstructive Surgery: March 2022 - 149 (3).

item.title: Filler Injection to the Mandibular Border. Video 4 from “A Novel Algorithm for Defining the Mandibular Border.” 149(3)
item.description:
所属期数: Ahead of Print
出版时间: Wed, 23 Feb 2022 17:21:03 GMT-06:00

Video demonstrates the augmentation of the mandibular border with filler injection. Slow antegrade and retrograde injections and are key maneuvers to avoid discomfort and bruising. Care should be taken to not over inject in this area as contour abnormalities can be quite visible. From “A Novel Algorithm for Defining the Mandibular Border.” Plastic and Reconstructive Surgery: March 2022 - 149(3).

Keywords: Mandibular Border; neuromodulator; filler; Liposuction; Radiofrequency; jawline

item.author: Rod J. Rohrich, MD
item.title: Neck Contouring. Video 3 from “A Novel Algorithm for Defining the Mandibular Border.” 149(3)
item.description:
所属期数: Ahead of Print
出版时间: Wed, 23 Feb 2022 17:23:06 GMT-06:00

Video demonstrates liposuction of the neck can be performed though a single 0.5cm submental incision. With adequate tumescent and materials, no general anesthesia is required. Radiofrequency ablation represents an emerging adjunct to liposuction that will help with improving post-operative skin tightening. The effect is to reduce the excess downward pull of the platysma on the skin over the mandible. From “A Novel Algorithm for Defining the Mandibular Border.” Plastic and Reconstructive Surgery: March 2022 - 149(3).

Keywords: Mandibular Border; neuromodulator; filler; Liposuction; Radiofrequency; jawline

item.description:
所属期数: Ahead of Print
出版时间: Wed, 23 Feb 2022 17:25:03 GMT-06:00

Video demonstrates Botulinum toxin injections into the lateral platysma and is shown here. Multiple injections of 5 units of product are injected along a horizontal line parallel to the mandibular border. Next, each platysmal band is injected with 5 units of toxin. The effect is to reduce the excess downward pull of the platysma on the skin over the mandible. From “A Novel Algorithm for Defining the Mandibular Border.” Plastic and Reconstructive Surgery: March 2022 - 149(3).

Keywords: Mandibular Border; neuromodulator; filler; Liposuction; Radiofrequency; jawline

item.author: Edward Chang
item.title: Lymphovenous bypass. Video 1 from “Practical Strategies in Reconstruction of Soft Tissue Sarcoma” November 2022 – 150 (5) CME
item.description:
所属期数: Ahead of Print
出版时间: Fri, 21 Oct 2022 20:45:29 GMT-05:00

The video demonstrates a lymphovenous bypass where an anastomosis is completed between a lymphatic vessel and recipient vein. The anastomosis allows the drainage of lymphatic fluid into the systemic circulation and has been proven to be remarkably effective in improving a patient’s lymphedema. Note the squares on the green silicone sheet used as a background for the operation which are each 1 x 1 mm to provide a size reference for the vessels that are repaired using this technique.
From “Practical Strategies in Reconstruction of Soft Tissue Sarcoma” November 2022– 150 (5). CME.
Keywords: sarcoma reconstruction, chest wall reconstruction, microvascular free flap, reconstructive ladder

item.description:
所属期数: Ahead of Print
出版时间: Mon, 27 Mar 2023 21:34:25 GMT-05:00

Video demonstrates that within 30 minutes of tourniquet inflation, the fascicle to ADM is stimulated with a Checkpoint nerve stimulator (Checkpoint Surgical Inc., Cleveland, OH). Contraction of ADM without stimulation of other ulnar intrinsics confirms this is the branch to ADM and the donor for transfer. From “Abductor Digiti Minimi and Anterior Interosseous Nerve to Ulnar Motor Nerve Transfer: The “Super-Turbocharge End to Side (STETS)” Transfer For Severe Ulnar Neuropathy.” Plastic and Reconstructive Surgery: April 2023 - 151(4).

item.description:
所属期数: Ahead of Print
出版时间: Mon, 24 Apr 2023 16:51:43 GMT-05:00

Video demonstrates Intra-operative ICG angiography depicting a patient with prior abdominal surgery and subsequent scar which prevented adequate perfusion to the lateral flap zone by the deep inferior epigastric system alone. Anastomoses of the SIEA/V to the DIEA/V in a dual-plane technique showed complete resolution and perfusion of this lateral zone, preventing fat necrosis post-operatively. item.author: Kevin Zuo
item.title: Active Movement Scale (AMS) overview. Video 1 from “RECENT ADVANCES IN THE TREATMENT OF BRACHIAL PLEXUS BIRTH INJURY” May 2023 – 151 (5) CME
item.description:
所属期数: Ahead of Print
出版时间: Mon, 24 Apr 2023 18:34:54 GMT-05:00

This video provides a brief overview of the Active Movement Scale (AMS) for infants and school-aged children with brachial plexus birth injury. The AMS evaluates active ROM relative to total passive ROM on a 7-point ordinal scale for a specific movement, both against gravity and with gravity eliminated. The AMS does not require the patient to follow commands, allows for extended statistical analysis, has excellent interrater reliability, and has been validated when used as a composite score (Toronto Test Score) to predict which BPBI patients would benefit from surgical intervention.

From “RECENT ADVANCES IN THE TREATMENT OF BRACHIAL PLEXUS BIRTH INJURY” May 2023 – 151 (5) CME.

Keywords: brachial plexus birth injury

item.author: Kevin Zuo
item.title: Shoulder examination. Video 2 from “RECENT ADVANCES IN THE TREATMENT OF BRACHIAL PLEXUS BIRTH INJURY” May 2023 – 151 (5) CME
item.description:
所属期数: Ahead of Print
出版时间: Mon, 24 Apr 2023 18:37:43 GMT-05:00

This video shows the examination of the shoulder in a child with brachial plexus birth injury is demonstrated. Soft tissue imbalances of the shoulder arise as a result of muscle denervation, incomplete reinnervation, and impaired longitudinal muscle growth. Serial clinical evaluation is fundamental for early recognition of glenohumeral pathology and early implementation of corrective strategies to preserve joint integrity and development.

From “RECENT ADVANCES IN THE TREATMENT OF BRACHIAL PLEXUS BIRTH INJURY” May 2023 – 151 (5) CME.

Keywords: brachial plexus birth injury

item.author: Kevin Zuo
item.title: Fibrin glue. Video 3 from “RECENT ADVANCES IN THE TREATMENT OF BRACHIAL PLEXUS BIRTH INJURY” May 2023 – 151 (5) CME
item.description:
所属期数: Ahead of Print
出版时间: Mon, 24 Apr 2023 18:43:01 GMT-05:00

This video demonstrates the use of fibrin glue without microsutures for interposition of nerve autografts in brachial plexus reconstruction. Our institution’s preference for the use of fibrin glue rather than microsutures is attributed to several advantages, including being more user friendly, less technically difficult, and more time efficient.

From “RECENT ADVANCES IN THE TREATMENT OF BRACHIAL PLEXUS BIRTH INJURY” May 2023 – 151 (5) CME.

Keywords: brachial plexus birth injury

item.author: John Semple
item.title: Pre-Operative Markings. Video 1 from “A Safe and Efficient Technique for Pedicled TRAM Flap Breast Reconstruction” June 2023 – 151 (6) CME
item.description:
所属期数: Ahead of Print
出版时间: Tue, 23 May 2023 21:14:42 GMT-05:00

This video describes preoperative markings for the TRAM Flap in a unilateral breast reconstruction. The video emphasizes the importance of using the contra lateral breast shape and contour as a guide to reconstructing the new breast and obtaining symmetry. The design of the Tram Flap on the lower abdomen is described outlining the main features of capturing the peri umbilical perforators. The video also shows the mastectomy scar being resected and placed in a vial to be sent for pathological analysis since the scar is one of the most common areas for recurrence.

From “A Safe and Efficient Technique for Pedicled TRAM Flap Breast Reconstruction” June 2023 – 151 (6) CME.

Keywords: breast reconstruction

item.title: Preparation of the Recipient Site on the Chest Wall. Video 2 from “A Safe and Efficient Technique for Pedicled TRAM Flap Breast Reconstruction” June 2023 – 151 (6) CME
item.description:
所属期数: Ahead of Print
出版时间: Tue, 23 May 2023 21:15:54 GMT-05:00

This video describes the preparation of the recipient site with elevation of the upper and lower flaps. The parameters of the medial and lateral extent of the reconstructed breast are identified and the importance of the placement of the inframammary fold emphasized.

The direction and location of the abdominal tunnel starting from the chest wall recipient site is also depicted.

From “A Safe and Efficient Technique for Pedicled TRAM Flap Breast Reconstruction” June 2023 – 151 (6) CME.

Keywords: breast reconstruction

item.author: John Semple
item.title: Raising the Abdominal Flap – Step I. Video 3 from “A Safe and Efficient Technique for Pedicled TRAM Flap Breast Reconstruction” June 2023 – 151 (6) CME
item.description:
所属期数: Ahead of Print
出版时间: Tue, 23 May 2023 21:17:06 GMT-05:00

The video describes the execution of the upper transverse incision of the Flap and the elevation of the upper abdominal tissue to the costal margin. Completion of the abdominal tunnel joining the chest wall recipient pocket is described emphasizing the medial/central alignment and the ideal width of the tunnel.

From “A Safe and Efficient Technique for Pedicled TRAM Flap Breast Reconstruction” June 2023 – 151 (6) CME.

Keywords: breast reconstruction

item.author: John Semple
item.title: Raising the Abdominal Flap – Step II. Video 4 from “A Safe and Efficient Technique for Pedicled TRAM Flap Breast Reconstruction” June 2023 – 151 (6) CME
item.description:
所属期数: Ahead of Print
出版时间: Tue, 23 May 2023 21:18:50 GMT-05:00

This video describes the next step in the elevation of the lower abdominal tissue starting with the lower transverse incision and the release of the umbilicus from the main body of the Flap. The contralateral side of the flap is raised first taking care to identify the location of the periumbilical perforators as they are encountered and transferring their positions onto the ipsilateral Flap with a marking pen. The ipsilateral flap is raised from lateral to medial to the lateral border of the rectus muscle. The video describes the elevation of the rectus muscle starting at the lateral border with the anterior fascia attached. The important anatomical features of the Deep Inferior Epigastric Vessels are illustrated.

From “A Safe and Efficient Technique for Pedicled TRAM Flap Breast Reconstruction” June 2023 – 151 (6) CME.

Keywords: breast reconstruction

item.author: John Semple
item.title: Raising the Abdominal Flap – Final Stage. Video 5 from “A Safe and Efficient Technique for Pedicled TRAM Flap Breast Reconstruction” June 2023 – 151 (6) CME
item.description:
所属期数: Ahead of Print
出版时间: Tue, 23 May 2023 21:20:25 GMT-05:00

This video describes the additional details of raising the rectus muscle taking care to note the position of the peri umbilical perforators. The medial border of the rectus muscle is identified and incised creating the rectus pedicle which extends to the xiphoid process. At the distal extent of the muscle pedicle the Deep Inferior Epigastric Vessels are located and ligated. The Flap is now entirely raised and dependent on the Rectus Muscle pedicle superiorly through the Superior Epigastric system. It is now completely freed from caudal to cephalad. The video shows the transfer of the completely raised flap superiorly through the tunnel through onto to the chest wall. Important orientation aspects of the Flap are described.

Details for in-setting of the Flap in the mastectomy site are shown. The video continues with addressing the closure of the rectus fascia defect as well as the abdominal skin flap closure. The sequence of the different steps of final in-setting of the Flap are described.

From “A Safe and Efficient Technique for Pedicled TRAM Flap Breast Reconstruction” June 2023 – 151 (6) CME.

Keywords: breast reconstruction

item.author: John Semple
item.title: Projection and In-setting of the Tram Flap Breast Reconstruction. Video 6 from “A Safe and Efficient Technique for Pedicled TRAM Flap Breast Reconstruction” June 2023 – 151 (6) CME
item.description:
所属期数: Ahead of Print
出版时间: Tue, 23 May 2023 21:22:51 GMT-05:00

This video describes the details using pictures of the TRAM Flap in-setting and how to control projection of the breast mound. The different tissue components are listed, and the area of de-epithelization required identified. The orientation of the skin paddle is shown and the in-setting of both the inframammary fold and the superior pole of the breast mound illustrated in sequence. Final closure details are reviewed with pictures and voice-over.

From “A Safe and Efficient Technique for Pedicled TRAM Flap Breast Reconstruction” June 2023 – 151 (6) CME.

Keywords: breast reconstruction

item.author: Edward I. Chang
item.title: Ulnar Artery Perforator Flap
item.description:
所属期数: Ahead of Print
出版时间: Wed, 28 Jun 2023 22:14:00 GMT-05:00

This video shows the Ulnar artery perforator flap harvest demonstrating markings and anatomic landmarks, but the most important factor to demonstrate in the video is the presence of a rare anatomic variant with a superficial ulnar artery. The ulnar neurovascular bundle is most commonly located deep, in between the flexor digitorum superficialis and flexor carpi ulnaris muscles. It is critical to be aware of this potential variant when harvesting the UAP flap, in addition to protection and careful dissection near the ulnar nerve.

item.author: Edward I. Chang
item.title: Medial Sural Artery Perforator Flap
item.description:
所属期数: Ahead of Print
出版时间: Wed, 28 Jun 2023 22:26:17 GMT-05:00

The medial sural artery perforator flap harvest is shown but demonstrates the freestyle nature of the flap anatomy. Despite the accepted landmarks guiding the flap design and the use of a handheld Doppler, the perforator location may not coincide with the landmarks. This is an important consideration when deciding to harvest the MSAP flap as the perforators are not as reliable as some of the other alternate flap donor sites presented. In this particular case, the perforator arose anterior to the incision and should be explored if the posterior dissection does not yield any usable perforators.

item.author: Edward I. Chang
item.title: Lateral Arm Perforator Flap
item.description:
所属期数: Ahead of Print
出版时间: Wed, 28 Jun 2023 22:29:27 GMT-05:00

The lateral arm perforator flap can be harvested reliably based on the landmarks for the perforator locations as shown. It is imperative to be cautious of the radial nerve during the pedicle dissection, and closure should not include re-approximation of the muscle or the deep fascia. Harvesting the flap based more distally can compensate for the shorter pedicle length and provide more pedicle length, but at the expense of flap volume as the flap becomes quite thin over the lateral epicondyle.

item.author: Edward I. Chang
item.title: Profunda Artery Perforator Flap
item.description:
所属期数: Ahead of Print
出版时间: Wed, 28 Jun 2023 22:34:20 GMT-05:00

The profunda artery perforator flap is best harvested in the longitudinal orientation with the patient in the supine frogleg position, and the dissection should begin posterior to the gracilis muscle to ensure the perforators are included in the flap harvest. The PAP can be harvested using a two-team approach, with a simultaneous harvest while the resection is performed without a position change. In certain circumstances, the perforator may join the gracilis pedicle allowing harvest of a chimeric gracilis PAP flap. There may be multiple perforators; however, they typically do not converge making the design of a chimeric or double skin paddle flap more difficult. The pedicle dissection to the takeoff from the profunda femoris vessels will often yield vessels of sufficient caliber and length.

item.author: Adel Bark Jr., MD
item.title: Muscle Component. Video 5 from “Multiplane L-Scar Augmentation Mastopexy: An Individualized Approach to Muscle, Glandular Tissue and Skin.” 153(4).
item.description:
所属期数: Ahead of Print
出版时间: Mon, 25 Mar 2024 23:36:02 GMT-05:00

Video demonstrates the last stage of the glandular tissue molding, where resections of the medial and lateral pillars are performed, first in the inferior horizontal direction and later in the medial vertical direction. On the vertical resection, it is important to make a horizontal incision in the breast tissue proximal to the areola, avoiding excess tissue at that location. From “Multiplane L-Scar Augmentation Mastopexy: An Individualized Approach to Muscle, Glandular Tissue and Skin.” Plastic and Reconstructive Surgery: April 2024 – 153(4).

Keywords: breast; Breast Implantation; Esthetics; Female; follow-up studies; Humans; mammoplasty; Mammaplasty / methods; Pectoralis Muscles / surgery; Postoperative Complications / epidemiology; Retrospective Studies; Treatment Outcome

item.author: Adel Bark Jr., MD
item.title: Skin Closure. Video 6 from “Multiplane L-Scar Augmentation Mastopexy: An Individualized Approach to Muscle, Glandular Tissue and Skin.” 153(4).
item.description:
所属期数: Ahead of Print
出版时间: Mon, 25 Mar 2024 23:43:11 GMT-05:00

Video demonstrates the first two stitches of the subdermal suture are the most important. First, we position the vertical incision where the scar should be, the first stitch will form the L angle and the second stitch over the vertical incision without performing skin compensation. From the second stitch we can start the compensations. From “Multiplane L-Scar Augmentation Mastopexy: An Individualized Approach to Muscle, Glandular Tissue and Skin.” Plastic and Reconstructive Surgery: April 2024 – 153(4).

Keywords: breast; Breast Implantation; Esthetics; Female; follow-up studies; Humans; mammoplasty; Mammaplasty / methods; Pectoralis Muscles / surgery; Postoperative Complications / epidemiology; Retrospective Studies; Treatment Outcome

item.description:
所属期数: Ahead of Print
出版时间: Tue, 28 May 2024 20:49:31 GMT-05:00

This video displays the Wide-Awake Bilateral Breast Reduction Surgery. This image from the middle of the surgery video shows the patient sitting up and able to see her first breast finished in the middle of the case. She sits up and goes home with zero nausea and vomiting at the end of the surgery, like she has been to the dentist.

From “New Frontiers in Wide Awake Surgery” June 2024 – 153 (6) CME.

Keywords: wide awake surgery

item.description:
所属期数: Ahead of Print
出版时间: Mon, 24 Jun 2024 22:13:56 GMT-05:00

Video demonstrates introduction of “rib plaster” to add volume to a SPF graft before the graft is inserted in the dorsum for discrete augmentation. Then, the use of “rib plaster” to add mild convexity to a SPLF graft already fixed to the dorsum is shown in another patient. Finally, the frequent use of rib plaster for minor tip definition-augmentation after skin closure is shown. From “Rib Plaster: a versatile, moldable derivative from scraping the periphery of the rib.” Plastic and Reconstructive Surgery: July 2024 – 154(1).

Keywords: rhinoplasty; rib graft; Rib plaster; Dorsum augmentation

item.author: Feras Alshomer, MBBS, MSc; Jeongmok Cho, MD; Changsik John Pak, MD; Hyunsuk Peter Suh MD, PhD; Joon Pio Hong MD, PhD, MMM
item.title: “Color Contrast” Technique with 10% Fuorescein Sodium Injection and Blue Marker Lymphatics Staining. Video from “‘Color Contrast’ Technique using 10 % Sodium Fluorescein and Blue Marker to Maximize Visualization During Lymphaticovenous Anastomosis.” 154-4
item.description:
所属期数: Ahead of Print
出版时间: Fri, 20 Sep 2024 00:22:51 GMT-05:00

Video demonstrates the “color contrast” technique with the use of 10% Fluorescein Sodium injection and blue marker lymphatics staining. Yellow stained lymphatics are clearly seen immediately after the dye injection prior to skin incision and after skin incision (green arrow). Lymphatic vessel identification and preparation followed by counter staining with blue marker is shown followed by lymphatic sidewall preparation for Side to End anastomosis. Clear visualization of the lymphatic wall during anastomosis process between the blue stained edge and yellow stained lumen with the use of double-color contrast technique is clearly seen (white arrow) aiding in assuring patency as confirmed by post anastomosis ICG imaging. From “‘Color Contrast’ Technique using 10 % Sodium Fluorescein and Blue Marker to Maximize Visualization During Lymphaticovenous Anastomosis.” Plastic and Reconstructive Surgery: October 2024 – 154(4).

Keywords: lymphedema; Lymphovenous anastomosis; side-to-end anastomosis; supermicrosurgery

item.description:
所属期数: Ahead of Print
出版时间: Mon, 28 Oct 2024 15:29:08 GMT-05:00

Video demonstrates Electroshaving. Bovie is in “cut” mode, energy is set between 6 and 8. A power diamond burr is used to smoothen bony and cartilaginous edges. Continuous irrigation with a solution containing 500 mL saline and 500 mg tranexamic acid allows removal of debris and efficient control of local bleeding. From “Low Strip Unifying Hybrid RhinoSeptoplasty (LUHRS): A Novel Classification in Dorsal Preservation Surgery.” Plastic and Reconstructive Surgery: November 2024 – 154(5).

Keywords: plastic surgery; preservation rhinoplasty; dorsal preservation; Hybrid rhinoplasty

item.author: R. Douglas Macmillan, MBChB, MD, FRCS; Kara Bell, BSc (Hons), PGDip HE; Emma Wilson, BSc(Hons), MPH, PGCert, PhD; Kristjan S. Asgeirsson, MS, FRCS
item.title: Explantation Mastopexy Auto-augment Technique. Video 1 from “Explantation with Lateral Pedicle Mastopexy.” 155(2).
item.description:
所属期数: Ahead of Print
出版时间: Tue, 21 Jan 2025 23:02:24 GMT-06:00

Video demonstrates the removal of the implant in the en-bloc fashion, with the blood supply persevered laterally. The breast is then divided medially to form a large extended lateral pedicle. The pedicle can then be rotated and folded towards the upper inner quadrant. The dermal surface of the pedicle can then be quilted in multiple rows to the pectoral fascia. The dermis of the pedicle is released to facilitate closure. The upper breast can then be closed. From “Explantation with Lateral Pedicle Mastopexy.” Plastic and Reconstructive Surgery: February 2025 – 155(2).

Keywords: breast; breast implant removal; mastopexy; ELPA; Explant with Lateral Pedicle Auto-augmentation; Post Augmentation Mastopexy; breast cancer; Female; Patient Satisfaction; retrospective; human; Patient Reported Outcome Measures

item.description:
所属期数: Ahead of Print
出版时间: Tue, 25 Feb 2025 16:01:35 GMT-06:00

This video displays a staged procedure in which the 1st webspace and index finger were released together, while simultaneously, a needle aponeurotomy was done to the little finger to extend it out of the palm for hygienic purposes. This was then followed by dynamic external fixation to address the little finger PIPJ contracture. The third stage of the surgery was a regional fasciectomy for the small and ring fingers.

From “Update on Dupuytren Disease - Pathogenesis, Natural History, Treatment, and Outcomes” February 2025 – 155 (3) CME.

Keywords: Dupuytren's Disease;treatment;flexion contracture

item.description:
所属期数: Ahead of Print
出版时间: Tue, 25 Feb 2025 16:03:47 GMT-06:00

This video demonstrates the operative technique: 1) medial crural footplate skeletonization and transection, 2) lateral steal and suturing of medial crural footplate to columellar strut graft and lateral crural footplates, and 3) removal of excess skin from columella. This patient example represents an extreme case of medial crura excision and we recommend gradual resection of the medial crura as needed for tip deprojection. From “Nasal Tip Deprojection in Rhinoplasty.” Plastic and Reconstructive Surgery: 3/1/2025 – 155 (3).

item.author: Chin-Ho Wong
item.title: Levator Advancement Technique for Ptosis Correction. Video from “Discussion: A Conservative Crease-Height-Adjustable Asian Double Eyelid Surgery Technique Based on Live Anatomical Studies.” 156 (1).
item.description:
所属期数: Ahead of Print
出版时间: Wed, 25 Jun 2025 15:49:43 GMT-05:00

This video demonstrates a standard levator advancement technique for ptosis correction. The pertinent anatomical landmarks of the Asian upper eyelid were clearly demonstrated here. Specifically, careful exposure of the anterior and posterior layers of the levator aponeurosis, the musculoaponeurotic junction of levator aponeurosis and the levator palpebrae superioris (LPS) and the upper edge of the tarsus is critical to precision in this procedure. From “Discussion: A Conservative Crease-Height-Adjustable Asian Double Eyelid Surgery Technique Based on Live Anatomical Studies.” Plastic and Reconstructive Surgery: 07/01/2025 – 156 (1).

item.description:

出版时间: Wed, 25 Jun 2025 17:18:04 GMT-05:00

Video shows a 34-year-old woman after right skin-sparing mastectomy with immediate prepectoral tissue expander (TE) placement plus acellular dermal matrix followed by 50 Gy of adjuvant chest wall radiation. item.description:
所属期数: Ahead of Print
出版时间: Thu, 24 Jul 2025 19:11:34 GMT-05:00

Video demonstrates B-mode and color flow mode ultrasound is used for identification of the perforator, through Scarpa’s fascia and terminating at the dermis level. Shortly after incision, the perforator can be identified within the subcutaneous fat, just deep to the dermis, from which point retrograde dissection along the perforator is performed until adequate pedicle length is achieved in patient 6. From “The Ultrathin Parascapular Flap: Proof of Concept and Use in Plantar Foot Reconstruction.” Plastic and Reconstructive Surgery: August 2025 – 156(2).

item.author: John M. Felder, MD
item.title: Harvest Technique Ultrathin Parascapular Free Flap. Video 3 from “The Ultrathin Parascapular Flap: Proof of Concept and Use in Plantar Foot Reconstruction.” 156(2).
item.description:
所属期数: Ahead of Print
出版时间: Thu, 24 Jul 2025 19:07:40 GMT-05:00

Video demonstrates ultrathin parascapular flap branches are identified in the subscarpal fat, the vessel is dissected antegrade towards the subdermal fat. In this case, even with pedicle harvest in the subcutaneous fat layer, vessel caliber was still sufficient for end to end anastomosis to the first dorsal metatarsal artery and vein in patient 5. From “The Ultrathin Parascapular Flap: Proof of Concept and Use in Plantar Foot Reconstruction.” Plastic and Reconstructive Surgery: August 2025 – 156(2).

Keywords: free flap; microsurgery; ultrathin flap; parascapular flap; Superthin flap; Perforator flap; suprascarpal flap; scapular flap; foot reconstruction; Diabetic Foot; wound reconstruction; Wound

item.author: John M. Felder, MD
item.title: Flap Harvest Without Ultrasound Planning–Ultrathin Parascapular Flap. Video 4 from “The Ultrathin Parascapular Flap: Proof of Concept and Use in Plantar Foot Reconstruction.” 156(2).
item.description:
所属期数: Ahead of Print
出版时间: Thu, 24 Jul 2025 18:59:34 GMT-05:00

Video demonstrates flap harvest technique for ultrathin parascapular flap without ultrasound. In this technique, the circumflex scapular vessels are initially approached in the traditional plane at the level of the triangular space, and antegrade dissection along the chosen branch is performed on patient 2. From “The Ultrathin Parascapular Flap: Proof of Concept and Use in Plantar Foot Reconstruction.” Plastic and Reconstructive Surgery: August 2025 – 156(2).

Keywords: free flap; microsurgery; ultrathin flap; parascapular flap; Superthin flap; Perforator flap; suprascarpal flap; scapular flap; foot reconstruction; Diabetic Foot; wound reconstruction; Wound

item.description:
所属期数: Ahead of Print
出版时间: Fri, 19 Dec 2025 17:42:48 GMT-06:00

Video demonstrates marking P points: P1, 6cm cranial to the inferior border of the NAC, P2 3 cm above the inferior border of the NAC and P3 just beneath the nipple. Sewing the parenchyma and the muscle using vertical Mastopexy principles to raise and stabilize the NAC. From “Customized Internal Mastopexy: How to stabilize the parenchyma in the Multiplane Mastopexy.” Plastic and Reconstructive Surgery: January 2026 – 157(1).


Keywords: breast; Augmentation Mastopexy; Internal Mastopexy; Multiplane L-Scar Mastopexy; Multiplane Raising of the IMF.

item.author: Shunichi Nomoto, MD, Ph.D., Rei Ogawa, MD, PhD, FACS
item.title: Ectropion-Free Transcutaneous Lower Blepharoplasty. Video from “Sub-tarsal Capsulopalpebral Fascia Incision: A New Paradigm for Ectropion-Free Transcutaneous Lower Blepharoplasty.” 157(4).
item.description:
所属期数: Ahead of Print
出版时间: Mon, 23 Mar 2026 18:24:46 GMT-05:00

Video demonstrates the septal reset procedure to prevent ectropion. After subperiosteal dissection and septum release, temporary fixation is made at three points. A sub-tarsal CPF incision (Nomoto’s incision; 1:30–2:00) with a horizontal cut 7 mm below the pre-tarsal orbicularis releases tension and prevents ectropion. Final sutures are placed evenly from 3 to 9 o’clock, with about 10 fixation points, paying attention to the lateral bulge. From “Sub-tarsal Capsulopalpebral Fascia Incision: A New Paradigm for Ectropion-Free Transcutaneous Lower Blepharoplasty.” Plastic and Reconstructive Surgery: April 2026 – 157(4).
Keywords: transcutaneous lower-eyelid surgery; ectropion; capsulopalpebral fascia; arcuate expansion; tear bags

item.author: Steven John Lo
item.title: The 3D Telemedicine System in Use. Video from “3D telemedicine outperforms 2D telemedicine: A randomized crossover trial in reconstructive plastic surgery.” 157 (5).
item.description:
所属期数: Ahead of Print
出版时间: Mon, 27 Apr 2026 18:28:54 GMT-05:00

Video demonstrates the 3D Telemedicine system in use. The clinician is ‘co-present’ with the patient in the same virtual clinic space. The patient’s 3D model can be annotated and drawn on. This helps the clinician to illustrate complex 3D reconstructive surgery and facilitates patient understanding. The patient can be viewed in 360 degrees in real time, facilitating bodypart assessment and if desired, aid remote physiotherapy. From “3D telemedicine outperforms 2D telemedicine: A randomized crossover trial in reconstructive plastic surgery.” Plastic and Reconstructive Surgery: Publication date – 157 (5).

Keywords: Telemedicine;free flap surgery;reconstructive;3D;remote consultation;surgery, plastic;patient preference;cross-over studies;informed consent
